jokes.text: I put a poisonous gas in one of these balloons, so if any of them pops, you may die.
jokes.setup: Leslie's cheerful balloon celebration for Ann's first day
jokes.punchline: April casually mentions potential death by poisonous gas
jokes.explanation: Perfect April character moment - dark humor delivered with complete deadpan sincerity, contrasting sharply with Leslie's enthusiasm. Strong character integration.

jokes.text: No, April, we would all die. Gases fill the volume of whatever container they're in.
jokes.setup: April's poisonous gas threat
jokes.punchline: Ann's matter-of-fact scientific correction completely missing the joke
jokes.explanation: Ann's practical nurse nature clashing with absurdist humor. Good character work but less memorable than April's line.

jokes.text: School.
jokes.setup: Ann's scientific explanation about gas physics
jokes.punchline: April's dismissive single-word response
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - one word that completely deflates Ann's earnestness. Classic April dismissal.

jokes.text: 11:00 a.m., 'The start-paperwork jamboree.' And then 12:00 noon sharp is the inaugural 'D-Ann-ce Party.'
jokes.setup: Leslie listing Ann's first day activities
jokes.punchline: Forced wordplay incorporating Ann's name into 'dance'
jokes.explanation: Typical Leslie over-enthusiasm with forced wordplay. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Get these (BLEEP) balloons out of here.
jokes.setup: Leslie's elaborate balloon celebration
jokes.punchline: Stuart's immediate hostile reaction as the grumpy office mate
jokes.explanation: Sets up Stuart as the expected grouch character. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: I think at some point, you and I should probably make out with each other. Yeah, good call.
jokes.setup: Normal work conversation about meetings
jokes.punchline: Casual transition to romantic suggestion treated like another work item
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection from mundane to romantic, delivered with perfect casualness. Shows their comfortable dynamic.

jokes.text: The best part of any relationship is the beginning. No problems, no fights. Just white wine, cuddling, and crazy amounts of History Channel documentaries.
jokes.setup: Ben talking about relationship phases
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly specific and nerdy romantic ideal with History Channel
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben character moment - romantic but with his signature nerdy specificity about History Channel.

jokes.text: Just a coincidence. We get it all the time. We laugh about it. But I've never met her. She's Filipino.
jokes.setup: Ben asking if Marlene Griggs-Knope is related to Leslie
jokes.punchline: Leslie's increasingly desperate lies that contradict each other
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy as Leslie digs herself deeper with contradictory lies. Sets up the episode's central deception.

jokes.text: You know what I should do? I should get my mother a one-way ticket to London, leaving today.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to avoid Ben meeting her mother
jokes.punchline: Dramatically over-the-top solution of shipping her mother to another continent
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie overreaction - solving simple problems with elaborate schemes. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Stuart, please, could you give us, like, 45 minutes? It's my office, too. Stuart.
jokes.setup: Ann politely asking Stuart to leave
jokes.punchline: Stuart's flat refusal and Ann's weak repeated plea
jokes.explanation: Shows Ann's conflict avoidance and Stuart's stubbornness. Awkward but not especially clever.

jokes.text: You are an intelligent, charismatic, beautiful superhero.
jokes.setup: Chris trying to motivate Jerry
jokes.punchline: Calling Jerry, the office incompetent, a 'beautiful superhero'
jokes.explanation: Good Chris character moment - his toxic positivity applied to Jerry. The irony is clear to everyone except Chris.

jokes.text: I just grab a few donuts, sit back, and enjoy the show.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining his attitude toward new city managers
jokes.punchline: His casual enjoyment of administrative chaos
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron philosophy - minimal effort, maximum entertainment from others' failures. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Is this a nightmare? April, wake up.
jokes.setup: Chris promoting April to multitasking executive aide
jokes.punchline: April's flat delivery suggesting this might be a bad dream
jokes.explanation: Great April moment - her horror at more responsibility delivered with perfect deadpan. The 'wake up' bit adds surreal comedy.

jokes.text: I did recently sell my Chronicles of Riddick DVD on eBay for $10. Used the profits to buy the Blu-ray.
jokes.setup: Chris praising Tom's entrepreneurial spirit
jokes.punchline: Tom's pathetically small 'business venture' upgrading his own movie
jokes.explanation: Good Tom character moment showing his delusions of grandeur applied to tiny transactions. The Riddick reference fits his taste.

jokes.text: I saw someone buy crystal meth out of a vending machine. It's a bad place.
jokes.setup: Tom complaining about the fourth floor
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning drug vending machines as evidence
jokes.explanation: Absurdist escalation that's both specific and ridiculous. The casual delivery sells the joke well.

jokes.text: I will give 110 percent! Well, as soon as you repeat yourself in a more interesting way.
jokes.setup: Andy promising maximum effort
jokes.punchline: Immediately revealing he wasn't listening and wants entertainment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy - enthusiastic but completely unfocused. The misdirection from commitment to entertainment request is well-crafted.

jokes.text: Looks like you got some shirt on your coffee.
jokes.setup: Ben spilling coffee on his shirt
jokes.punchline: Marlene's deliberate reversal of the expected phrase
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that immediately establishes Marlene as sharp and slightly intimidating. Good economy.

jokes.text: Fun anecdote.
jokes.setup: Ben's awkward explanation of his coffee spill
jokes.punchline: Marlene's dry dismissal calling his nervous rambling a 'fun anecdote'
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan putdown. Shows Marlene's no-nonsense attitude and Ben's nervousness affecting his competence.

jokes.text: Then I'm confused, Ben, because you just told me it was. Five seconds ago.
jokes.setup: Ben contradicting himself about school bus feasibility
jokes.punchline: Marlene pointing out his immediate flip-flop with precision
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Ben's complete flustering under Marlene's pressure. Sets up his pushover reputation.

jokes.text: This desk is the epitome of the Swedish concept of jämställdhet, or 'equality.'
jokes.setup: Chris introducing Ron's new circular desk
jokes.punchline: Over-explaining Swedish philosophy for a ridiculous desk setup
jokes.explanation: Classic Chris over-intellectualizing simple things. The Swedish word adds pretentious absurdity fitting his character.

jokes.text: Swivel! What is it, Jerry? You told me to say your name. And you did a great job, superstar.
jokes.setup: Chris demonstrating his swivel desk functionality
jokes.punchline: The absurd theater of swiveling to Jerry who was just following instructions
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy of the swivel combined with Chris's misplaced enthusiasm for Jerry's simple compliance.

jokes.text: You look like a freak. Swivel!
jokes.setup: Chris asking for more demonstrations
jokes.punchline: April's brutal honesty followed by Chris's enthusiastic swivel response
jokes.explanation: Perfect April directness combined with Chris's obliviousness. The physical swivel makes his missing the insult even funnier.

jokes.text: I loved that pillar. It made it really annoying to stand in my doorway.
jokes.setup: Chris removing obstacles to make Ron more accessible
jokes.punchline: Ron revealing he specifically enjoyed being less accessible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - finding value in obstacles to human interaction. Great character consistency with his anti-social preferences.

jokes.text: There's a whole room on the fourth floor where they store the knives they've confiscated from people who went to the fourth floor to stab someone.
jokes.setup: Tom continuing his fourth floor complaints
jokes.punchline: Circular logic of knife confiscation from people who came there to stab
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic - the fourth floor is so bad it creates its own cycle of violence and confiscation.

jokes.text: Hello, Wall Street. Buy more stocks. Now.
jokes.setup: Andy getting excited about his office phone
jokes.punchline: His childish understanding of how business/Wall Street works
jokes.explanation: Pure Andy naivety - his cartoonish idea of how financial markets work. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: This phone kind of smells like a butt. Try not to move things around. Because technically speaking, this is still a crime scene.
jokes.setup: Andy commenting on the phone smell
jokes.punchline: Ethel's casual revelation that it's an active crime scene
jokes.explanation: Great dark escalation from gross smell to crime scene. Ethel's matter-of-fact delivery sells the absurdity.

jokes.text: Remind me next time to ask her where she was when Lincoln got shot.
jokes.setup: Ethel being difficult about the name tags
jokes.punchline: Tom suggesting Ethel is old enough to have witnessed Lincoln's assassination
jokes.explanation: Standard 'person is really old' joke elevated slightly by the specific historical reference and Tom's frustration.

jokes.text: And I definitely heard your feedback from last time, so I only put five Sarah McLachlan songs on this one.
jokes.setup: Leslie making Ben a romantic mix
jokes.punchline: Thinking five Sarah McLachlan songs is showing restraint
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie character moment - her earnest attempt at romance being tone-deaf. The specific artist choice fits.

jokes.text: I mean, at one point, for no reason, I just took off my shoes and held them in my hand.
jokes.setup: Ben describing how badly his meeting went
jokes.punchline: Bizarre, inexplicable nervous behavior of removing shoes
jokes.explanation: Excellent specific absurd nervous behavior. The 'for no reason' makes it even funnier - pure anxiety response.

jokes.text: He's like a goofy, spineless jellyfish.
jokes.setup: Marlene describing Ben to Leslie (unknowingly)
jokes.punchline: Mixing 'spineless' literally and figuratively with jellyfish comparison
jokes.explanation: Good visual metaphor combining spinelessness with actual jellyfish anatomy. Sets up Ben's confidence arc.

jokes.text: When I first met you, I thought you were a fascist hard-ass. What? A cute fascist hard-ass.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to reassure Ben about not being a pushover
jokes.punchline: Adding 'cute' to soften the fascist insult
jokes.explanation: Leslie's attempt to be helpful making things worse. The 'cute' qualifier is endearing but misses the point.

jokes.text: Or we could go to Belize and go scuba diving. And we could look at the whale sharks. You're certified, right? Let's get you certified.
jokes.setup: Ben offering to confront Marlene again
jokes.punchline: Immediately spiraling into elaborate escape fantasy
jokes.explanation: Good Ben character moment - his tendency to escape into elaborate fantasies when stressed. The specificity helps.

jokes.text: Sir, are you aware that there is waste in your water system?
jokes.setup: Citizen complaining about sprinkler water making him sick
jokes.punchline: Jerry completely ignoring him while dealing with his circular desk
jokes.explanation: Shows the dysfunction of Chris's changes - real citizen issues ignored for desk gimmicks. Functional but not standout.

jokes.text: Hello. Wrong. What? No preambles. No introductions. Just walk in and start talking.
jokes.setup: Leslie coaching Ben to be more assertive
jokes.punchline: Immediately shutting down his polite greeting as weakness
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Leslie's coaching becoming more aggressive than helpful. Shows her misunderstanding of confidence.

jokes.text: 'Um' is the sound in 'dumb.' That's what she says to people.
jokes.setup: Ben saying 'um' during practice
jokes.punchline: Leslie quoting her mother's harsh wordplay putdown
jokes.explanation: Shows Marlene's verbal cruelty through Leslie's traumatized recollection. The wordplay is mean but clever.

jokes.text: Here is a list of my mother's top 100 favorite conversation topics, starting with Persian rugs, ending with Daniel Craig.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to prepare Ben for her mother
jokes.punchline: The absurd specificity of having 100 ranked topics with random endpoints
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie over-preparation with specific absurd details. Persian rugs to Daniel Craig is a perfect random range.

jokes.text: Tell Mort I said 'Your move.'
jokes.setup: Someone looking for Mort Jansen's office
jokes.punchline: Cryptic chess-like challenge message delivered casually
jokes.explanation: Good mysterious government office humor. The chess reference suggests ongoing bureaucratic warfare.

jokes.text: How? By shining down on them with the Haverford charm ray.
jokes.setup: Tom planning to get ladies to do his work
jokes.punchline: Describing his charm as a literal ray gun with sound effects
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom delusion - treating his perceived charisma as a superpower. The physical ray gun gesture adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: Julianne Moore just called. She wants her hair back. Nobody named Julien called.
jokes.setup: Tom's elaborate compliment comparing Ethel to actress Julianne Moore
jokes.punchline: Ethel's literal interpretation completely missing the reference
jokes.explanation: Great generational/cultural clash. Tom's smooth line completely lost on Ethel who takes it literally.

jokes.text: And, two, will you please invite me to your 30th birthday party?
jokes.setup: Tom continuing to charm elderly Ethel
jokes.punchline: Suggesting the clearly elderly woman is turning 30
jokes.explanation: Standard age flattery joke. Works for Tom's character but not particularly original or memorable.

jokes.text: Maybe I'll just find an open window and plummet to my death. Okay.
jokes.setup: Ron frustrated with his desk situation
jokes.punchline: Casually suggesting suicide, Andy's cheerful acceptance
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Ron's dramatic despair met by Andy's oblivious acceptance. Dark humor fits both characters.

jokes.text: I'm down to one word a minute, and the word is 'perflipisklup' because I can't fly spaceships.
jokes.setup: Ron complaining about his circular keyboard
jokes.punchline: Made-up word and spaceship metaphor for his typing inability
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ron frustration with specific absurd details. 'Perflipisklup' is a great made-up word, spaceship analogy fits the futuristic desk.

jokes.text: But this is Chris Traeger, the six million dollar man. He won't quit.
jokes.setup: Ron suggesting Chris will give up like other managers
jokes.punchline: Donna comparing Chris to the bionic TV character for his persistence
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that fits Chris's superhuman energy levels. Establishes his uniqueness among city managers.

jokes.text: Wow, maybe you should talk to him, Mom. He seems pretty self-assured.
jokes.setup: Ben being aggressive with Marlene during their coffee
jokes.punchline: Leslie's proud misreading of the hostile situation
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - Leslie thinks her coaching worked while we see Ben being inappropriately aggressive.

jokes.text: Who was that? I don't know. I saw her crying, and so I helped.
jokes.setup: Jerry comforting a random crying woman
jokes.punchline: His simple, kind impulse with no context needed
jokes.explanation: Sweet Jerry moment showing his genuine kindness. The simplicity of his motivation is touching and funny.

jokes.text: Let your brain unlock the door to your heart's future. I made that expression up when I was 14. Still in use today. By me.
jokes.setup: Chris giving Tom inspirational advice
jokes.punchline: Admitting he invented his own quote as a teenager and still uses it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris moment - his earnest self-help philosophy exposed as teenage creation. The 'By me' admission is endearingly honest.

jokes.text: 'You need a lobotomy. I'll get a saw.' I love Calvin and Hobbes. Me, too!
jokes.setup: Ben and Marlene having a tense negotiation
jokes.punchline: Bonding over Calvin and Hobbes despite the hostile quote
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection from hostile negotiation to shared nostalgia. Shows how pop culture can bridge conflicts.

jokes.text: You two are just the cutest things on four legs! But why so much black? It's like you're going to a funeral.
jokes.setup: Marlene meeting Ben and Leslie together
jokes.punchline: Treating them like pets then criticizing their clothing choices
jokes.explanation: Shows Marlene's condescending nature - infantilizing them then immediately criticizing. Sets up her difficult personality.

jokes.text: Stop laughing, Muriel. Stop it!
jokes.setup: Tom making jokes about Ethel to the other elderly workers
jokes.punchline: Ethel's frustrated attempts to control her colleague's laughter
jokes.explanation: Shows Ethel losing control of the situation as Tom's charm actually works on someone else. Functional workplace comedy.

jokes.text: Your mom kind of, um, made a pass at me.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to get Ben to give her mom a gift
jokes.punchline: Ben's uncomfortable revelation about Marlene's inappropriate behavior
jokes.explanation: Major plot escalation delivered with perfect awkwardness. Creates the episode's central crisis for the secret relationship.

jokes.text: So, Webster's dictionary describes a pork as a... No, it's park... Park. I'm sorry. Geez, okay.
jokes.setup: Jerry trying to give a professional briefing
jokes.punchline: Immediately messing up the first word and flustering
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry incompetence under pressure. The park/pork mix-up is exactly what would happen to him in this role.

jokes.text: You have come up with a plan so spectacularly horrible that it might ruin the entire department. Now, wait a minute. I mean that as a compliment.
jokes.setup: Ron seeming to criticize Chris's management
jokes.punchline: Revealing he appreciates the destructive efficiency
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and perfect Ron character moment. His appreciation for spectacularly efficient failure is both funny and insightful.

jokes.text: And you shine a light on him, and he shrinks up faster than an Eskimo's scrotum.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining Jerry's performance issues
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and crude metaphor for Jerry's reaction to attention
jokes.explanation: Peak Ron metaphor - crude, specific, and perfectly descriptive. The image is both shocking and accurate for Jerry's character.

jokes.text: I may have a compromise.
jokes.setup: Ron's entire anti-compromise philosophy
jokes.punchline: The man who hates compromise offering one as his only solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Ron doing something completely against his nature shows how desperate the situation is.

jokes.text: How were you dressed? Oh, my God.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking if Ben was 'asking for' her mother's advances
jokes.punchline: Ben's horror at being blamed for the inappropriate behavior
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Leslie's panic making her say terrible things. Ben's reaction sells the absurdity.

jokes.text: Four buses. Two. Deal.
jokes.setup: The entire episode's negotiation over school buses
jokes.punchline: Ben successfully negotiating down from her original position
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the episode's B-plot. Shows Ben finding his confidence and getting results despite the chaos.

jokes.text: A year ago, I lived in a pit. Now I got a job, and a kickass wife. And my band is so good, and are you gonna eat that pecan square?
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his happiness philosophy
jokes.punchline: Transitioning from gratitude speech to food interest mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - genuine gratitude undercut by his inability to focus. The pit reference is great continuity.

jokes.text: When life gives me lemons, I'm gonna slice them up into wedges and throw them into vodka tonics, which I will then sip in a burlesque nightclub that I co-own with actor Taye Diggs and two of the Pussycat Dolls.
jokes.setup: April rejecting the standard lemonade philosophy
jokes.punchline: Elaborately specific alternative involving nightclub ownership with celebrities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant April moment - rejecting optimism for an absurdly specific hedonistic fantasy. The celebrity choices are perfect random specificity.

jokes.text: Citizen request. Swivel, swivel, swivel. Hello. I can help you in here, sir.
jokes.setup: Chris still stuck using his circular desk system
jokes.punchline: The continued absurdity of the swiveling bureaucracy
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier desk comedy but with diminishing returns. Shows Chris's persistence but isn't as funny the second time.

jokes.text: Everything I built. Jump up here. I'll show you how it's done. See, your problem is you're thinking too much.
jokes.setup: Customer complaining about Andy's shoe shine technique
jokes.punchline: Taking over to demonstrate while giving business advice
jokes.explanation: Good role reversal with customer taking charge. The irony of giving Andy advice while doing his job is solid.

jokes.text: What a moron.
jokes.setup: Customer helping Andy and giving him advice
jokes.punchline: Andy completely missing that he's the incompetent one
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy obliviousness - completely missing the obvious irony. Great economy with just two words for maximum character moment.
